other_observers: Jim & Karen Schmoker, Charlene & Garrett Schmoker, Peter Gent, Glenn Walbek, Steve Mlodinow, et al.; I found and originally identified the bird.
description: Grayish finch the size of American Goldfinch with small yellow bill and cheery red cap. Underparts mostly white with pale pink suffusion on upper breast, extending slightly into forward portion of flanks. Flanks with diffuse discontinuous pale gray streaking, heaviest streak rearmost, often not visible under wing and stronger on bird’s right side. Undertail coverts white except for gray streak on longest undertail covert. Tail mainly seen from underneath, pale gray. Upper surface of tail darker gray with whitish fringes. Rump very clean, mostly white with some pink tinge towards tail. Wings grayish with wide white bar formed at tip of greater coverts. Back grayish with a faint tinge of beige, overall pale color formed by wide pale fringes on mantle feathers. Head with a thick-necked profile, similar gray-beige as mantle overall. Lores and chin medium gray, separated by pale feathers in-between to create discontinuous subtle mask. Nasal feathering extending down base of maxilla creates a bumped look on forehead. Pale line extends from above eye to forehead, separating mask from red cap. Cherry-red cap well defined, smallish, extends only slightly behind eye.Behaviors: Feeding on Nyjer seed with Common Redpolls (up to about 20), American Goldfinches, and House Finches.Call: Not notedPlumage: Adult male exilipes ssp
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
